rationale: GROUND TRUTH: control case (no misstatement; scheme fields all null). DIM1: Output states misstatement_probability=55. Control rubric: p>=50 -> 0. Score 0. DIM2: Control case -> null per rubric (mechanism hypotheses are not graded for controls). DIM3: Control case -> nulls per rubric. DIM4: The evidence is largely specific and arithmetically sound: e.g., CL3 cites NetIncomeLoss=7,372,756 vs OCF=-2,453,218 (FY2013, accession 0001144204-15-012563); CL5 assembles 5-year NI ($7.4M-$9.8M), revenue ($228.5M-$286.2M), and OCF ($-2.45M to $18.0M) series that genuinely support the smoothing/divergence claims; CL1's growth-rate math checks out (AR +13.7% vs revenue +16.9%). The mechanism assertions on this control case are framed hypothetically and anchored to these same concrete figures. Consistency rule: p=55 requires watch/elevated; declared risk_tier='elevated' -> consistent, no cap. However, full score 3 is withheld because CL7 and mechanism hypothesis 3 assert a '10-K/A filed 2013-03-05, one day after original 10-K' while citing accession 0001144204-13-012622 - which per documents_used is the original 10-K itself (filed 2013-03-04); no 10-K/A appears in documents_used, making this specific citation unverifiable/possibly fabricated. Score 2 (specific data points genuinely support most claims, but the multi-point synthesis includes one unsupported citation; flagged for human auditor). MEMORIZATION_SUSPECT_CONDITION2: false - the reasoning repeatedly points at concrete provided data content (specific XBRL-style figures with accessions and locations) rather than merely listing documents, and it mentions no post-cutoff facts (no revelation, enforcement, or outcome references); the questionable 10-K/A item is a chronology claim within the document window, not a post-cutoff fact.
